header: Gerente de People & Culture — Maple Bear
hub_subtitle: Business English para People & Culture, comunicação executiva e a liderança da divisão do Canadá

lesson.menu_title: Diagnostic Session -- Who Is Danielle in English?
lesson.menu_desc: Executive self-positioning in People & Culture -- 34 slides
lesson.subtitle: Lesson 1 -- Diagnostic + Executive Self-Positioning
lesson.title_tag: Professor View -- Danielle Moreira | Lesson 1 -- Diagnostic + Executive Self-Positioning

listenings.text: Danielle, this is Nathan Boyd, Vice President of Academic Operations at Maple Bear Canada. Welcome aboard. I wanted to leave you a proper introduction before we meet in August. Our academic team here is small but very senior. Most of them hold doctorates in education, and several of them have been with the franchisor since the very first Canadian school opened. They are curious about the work you have been leading in Brazil, and they have already asked me for the employee journey framework you built there. Now, a word of advice. Our senior leadership will not expect you to arrive with all the answers. They will expect you to ask very good questions. So take a stance, but leave room for their input. I will set up thirty minute one on one sessions with each of the leaders in your first two weeks. Talk soon.

Good morning, everyone, and thank you for joining this global people update. I want to open with the one number that matters. Ninety one percent of our people say they understand how their work connects to our strategy. Two years ago that number was fifty four percent. That is what alignment looks like when it is done properly. This year we are rolling out the new employee journey across every region, and headquarters will not do it for you. Each market owns its own culture. My team owns the framework. We are making headway on the hybrid model as well, although I will be honest with you, we are still learning. And a final word to our senior leadership. Culture is not a project that finishes. It is the way we behave when nobody is measuring us.

items.situation: "So, tell us who you are and what you do."
tips: I lead People and Culture at...

items.situation: "How long have you been doing this kind of work?"
tips: I've been leading culture transformation since...
I've worked in People and Culture for...

items.situation: "What exactly are you working on right now?"
tips: At the moment, we're rolling out...
present continuous -- in progress, not permanent

items.situation: "What does Maple Bear actually do? Explain it to an outsider."
tips: We're a franchisor in bilingual education.
We license our model to local operators.

items.situation: "Why should our academic team listen to you?"
tips: I'm not here to import a Brazilian model.
What I bring is a framework, not a finished answer.

items.situation: "What is the first thing you'll do when you land in Canada?"
tips: The first thing I want to do is carry out a cultural assessment.
Before I propose anything, I want to listen.
